mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales month-over-month reflect accelerating consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when RSAFS increases month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals labor market deterioration, reducing household income expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the UNRATE trigger fires during any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low nominal interest rates and positive consumer price acceleration generate negative real yields, channeling capital from fixed income into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal instances fail to outperform single-signal FEDFUNDS-only instances by at least 3 percentage points annualized over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal capacity contraction and impending earnings compression, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during late-cycle periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST month-over-month changes coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0 percent restricts credit conditions and raises equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in nonfarm payrolls and increases in the unemployment rate confirm sustained labor market weakening, reducing aggregate demand and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change negative and UNRATE rises over same window
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly acceleration in real GDP growth signals improving macro fundamentals and expanding corporate profit potential, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Accelerating GDPC1 coincides with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations over any 5-year rolling window.
